metadata.name: z-zephyr
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
metadata.summary: Zephyr is a declarative neural network library on top of JAX allowing for easy and fast neural network designing, creation, and manipulation

modules.name: zephyr
documentation:
zephyr
A neural network library on top of JAX allowing for easy and fast neural network designing, creation, and manipulation

Early Stage

documentation: TODO: Decide if these should go in _nets: they are increasingly useful in creation of nets

modules.name: zephyr.functools.partial
documentation:
Module that would be possibly helpful for partial
application of functions in python


Notes:
    - experimental: might or might not be useful for the 
    rest of the lib
    - hopefully: it helps create more readable code

functions.name: zephyr.functools.partial.hole_aware
documentation: doing: replace REPLACEMENT OF HOLES with CALLING OF HOLES

methods.name: zephyr.building.skeleton.Skeleton.materialize
documentation: This initializes the arrays at the leaves using the appropriate initializers

modules.name: zephyr._nets.composition
documentation: what are good ways to compose functions relevant for DNNs?

functions.name: zephyr._nets.linear.branch_linear
documentation:
Branches the last dimension of `x` with each branch having the same dimension as the last dimension of `x`

Example:
    if x.shape == (..., e) then after this function

        z = branch_linear (...x, num_branches...)
        z.shape == (..., num_branches, x.shape[-1])
